footer.hours_placeholder: Hours to be confirmed from Google Business Profile.

home.hero_kicker: Stylish unisex hair studio · Warsaw Wilcza 26 · dog-friendly
home.pillars_title: Every visit starts with a conversation about <accent>what you really need</accent>
home.pillar_on_desc: Classic men's cuts. Modern and bold forms: mullet, wolf cut, chelsea. Fade, taper fade, beards and repigmentation.
home.pillar_ona_desc: Classic women's cuts and modern forms. Short cuts, fringes and an individual take on style. Color and perm consultations.
home.pillar_vibe_desc: Pop-ups, collabs and events. Music, fashion, people and good energy. Once in a while the studio turns into a space for meetings, shopping and shared vibe.
home.reviews_proof: 5.0 average from 305 reviews, data from Booksy
home.magazines_intro: We host issues from newonce and Aktivist. Drop by, grab a copy or read on the spot with a coffee.
home.magazines_newonce_desc: Magazine and radio about music, fashion and culture.
home.popular_intro: A quick door into what people search for at our place most often.

faq.q: Where is FRIME in Warsaw?
faq.a: Wilcza 26, 00-544, Śródmieście Południowe district. A few minutes' walk from Centrum and Politechnika metro stations, one turn from the Krucza tram stop.

faq.q: Is this a men's or women's studio?
faq.a: Unisex. We do men's cuts, beards and repigmentation, and women's wolf cuts, fringes and color consultations. The menu uses HIM and HER labels, but each of the three of us covers both.

faq.q: How do I book?
faq.a: Fastest through Booksy, link in the header and footer. You can also message us on Instagram. Phone works during opening hours.

faq.q: Can I bring my dog?
faq.a: Yes, dogs are welcome. We have a water bowl and a corner where your dog can lie down during the visit.

services.on_intro: Cut tailored to you. Classic or something bolder - it all starts with a consultation.
services.ona_intro: Classic and modern forms. Short cuts, fringes and an individual approach - every visit starts with a consultation.

faq_on.q: Do I need to book or can I just walk in?
faq_on.a: Booking is safer. During the week we usually run full a few days ahead, walk-in slots are rare. The fastest way to book is through Booksy.

faq_on.q: Do you only cut men's hair?
faq_on.a: No. FRIME is a unisex studio. We do both HIM and HER cuts, each of the three of us handles both. The category names are only there to make the price list easier to read.

faq_on.q: What is beard and head repigmentation?
faq_on.a: It's a treatment where we use a specific pigment to fill in patchy beard areas or push back a receding hairline. It lasts a few weeks, fades naturally and doesn't damage the skin. Price: 80 PLN, time: thirty minutes.
Yes, dogs are welcome. We have a water bowl and a corner where your dog can lie down. If your dog is nervous in new places, mention it when you book and we'll pick a quieter slot.
Whatever works. We have a terminal, accept BLIK and cash. We can issue a company invoice, just let us know before payment.

faq_ona.a: We do the color appointment itself after a free consultation. The consultation is 15 minutes and lets us agree on what you want, what's actually achievable on your hair and how long the appointment will take. Price for the color itself depends on length and what we're doing.

faq_ona.q: How long does a short cut take?
faq_ona.a: Between sixty and seventy-five minutes, depending on starting length and exactly what we're cutting. You get the specific time and price in the first minute of the visit, before we start.

faq_ona.q: Is a fringe trim a separate visit?
faq_ona.a: Yes, a stand-alone fringe trim is 30 minutes and 80 PLN. If the fringe is part of a full cut, we do it inside that appointment.

faq_ona.q: Do you have your own care products?
faq_ona.a: We use a few trusted brands in the studio and only recommend products we use ourselves. If you want to take something home, ask your stylist after the visit and we'll point you to what actually works for your hair type.
Yes, also after a consultation. A perm is a decision you can't undo in five minutes, so we always sit down first and check whether it even makes sense for your hair structure.

team.title: Team
team.intro: We build a place with an individual take on style. Each of us works differently, but we share an approach to form and aesthetics.

julia.name: Julia
julia.bio: Julia is the person behind the FRIME idea. She cuts both men's and women's hair. She loves working with texture, mullets and strong fringes. A full bio with backstory will land here once we've sat down with the friend.

sasza.name: Sasza
sasza.bio: Sasza joined FRIME early. Specializes in strong fades and beard work, though in a unisex studio nobody is fenced in by labels, so women's cuts are equally on the menu. Full bio coming after a chat.

kara.name: Kara
kara.bio: Kara is the newer face on the team, but quickly built up a steady group of regulars. Most comfortable in short cuts and strong fringes. The rest of the bio we'll add together.

alex.name: ALEX
alex.bio: ALEX joined FRIME in 2025. Works both men's and women's cuts. Full bio coming soon.

inga.name: INGA
inga.bio: INGA joined FRIME in 2025. Does women's and men's cuts, loves strong forms and color work. Full bio coming soon.

vibe.title: VIBE
vibe.intro: Once a year, sometimes twice, we do something that isn't a haircut. A vinyl pop-up, a DJ set for our birthday, a collab with a local brand. Here's the archive of what's happened and a hint of what's next.
vibe.next_placeholder: The next event will be announced as soon as it's confirmed. Come back in a few weeks or follow us on Instagram, that's where we post first.

akademia.title: ACADEMY
akademia.intro: We're working on a training program for people who want to learn to cut the FRIME way. Short weekend courses, intensives, private one-on-ones. Drop your email and you'll be the first to know.
what_we_plan_list: Intensives for working stylists who want to extend their technique
Private one-on-one sessions tailored to your level
Occasional guest talks from the Polish and international scene

blog.title: Reading room
blog.intro: We write about what's happening in the studio and around it. Cut trends, behind-the-scenes of pop-ups, short guides on home care. One piece a week, no noise.
blog.empty: The first piece will land here soon.

kontakt.title: Contact
kontakt.how_to_get: We're at Wilcza 26, right in the middle of Warsaw's Śródmieście. The closest metro stops are Centrum (line M1) and Politechnika, both a ten-minute walk away. Trams 4, 18 and 35 stop at Krucza, one block away. Parking nearby is paid, the easiest spots are usually on Mokotowska.

about.title: About
about.lede: FRIME started in 2025 at Wilcza 26, in the middle of Warsaw's Śródmieście. The idea was simple: build a hair studio where you don't have to decide whether you're going to a men's barber or a women's salon. We cut anyone who wants to look good walking out, and we don't pretend one of those categories is more important than the other. The team is three people: Julia, Sasza and Kara. We run three stations and only take a limited number of clients at a time, so nobody feels like they're on a conveyor belt. Color and perm consultations are always free, dogs welcome, card and BLIK accepted.
about.value_1_desc: We don't split cuts into men's and women's by default. We do what you want on your head.
about.value_2_desc: Your dog gets their own corner and a bowl of water. Just let us know when you book.
about.value_3_desc: A visit lasts as long as it needs to. Color and perm consultations are always free.
about.proof_desc: That's the rating on our Booksy profile. Every person who left a review was actually here. We don't buy reviews and we won't ask you for one on your way out. If you want to recommend us, the best thing is to send a friend who needs it.

legal.privacy_intro: Short version, because we don't collect much.

privacy_sections.title: Who processes your data
privacy_sections.text: The data controller is FRIME, Wilcza 26, 00-544 Warsaw. For privacy questions: the email address in the page footer.

privacy_sections.title: What we collect and why
privacy_sections.text: On this site we use cookie-less analytics (Plausible or a similar tool). It records anonymous traffic stats: which page, which country, which screen size. We don't link this data to you. Bookings are handled by an external service, Booksy, whose policy is on their own site.

privacy_sections.title: How long we keep it
privacy_sections.text: Traffic stats are kept for 12 months. Booking data is stored by Booksy according to their policy.

privacy_sections.title: Your rights
privacy_sections.text: You have the right to access your data, correct it, delete it and transfer it. You can also object to processing at any time. Email us and we respond within 30 days.

privacy_sections.title: Complaint
privacy_sections.text: If you believe we process your data unlawfully, you can file a complaint with the President of the Polish Data Protection Authority (UODO).

legal.privacy_note: This is a working draft tuned to our current stack. Before publishing we ask a friendly lawyer to read it through.
legal.terms_intro: A short note on what this site is and how it works.

terms_sections.title: Who runs this site
terms_sections.text: The site is run by FRIME, Wilcza 26, 00-544 Warsaw. All content, photos and graphics are ours or used with the authors' consent.

terms_sections.title: What it's for
terms_sections.text: The site presents the studio, our team, services and events. Bookings happen on Booksy. Prices can change, the current version is always on our Booksy profile.

terms_sections.title: External links
terms_sections.text: We link to Booksy, Instagram, TikTok and other partner services. We're not responsible for the content of pages we link to.

terms_sections.title: Contact
terms_sections.text: Questions, notes, correction requests: reach us on Instagram or at the email in the footer.

legal.cookies_intro: This site doesn't use tracking cookies.
legal.cookies_body: Our analytics tool doesn't store cookies in your browser and doesn't identify you as a user. That's why you don't see a cookie consent banner here. The only data we collect is anonymous traffic stats: country, screen size, which pages get the most visits. We don't use ad pixels or Google Analytics either. If that ever changes, we'll update this text and add a proper consent banner.

bvclub-kiosk-popup.title: FRIME × BVCLUB × KIOSK
bvclub-kiosk-popup.body: 06/06. Pop-Up store at the studio. Vintage, leather, DJ-set, FREE DRINKS (0% ALCO). 13:00-20:00, Warsaw, Wilcza 26. BVCLUB and KIOSK are two independent brands we team up with to turn the studio into a one-day space of vintage, fashion and music. A poster with a row of mannequins and a line that stuck with us: it doesn't matter who you are, everyone needs clothes and a haircut.

popup-popiloni-maki-yoco.title: POP-UP × POPILONI × MAKI × YOCO
popup-popiloni-maki-yoco.body: On a Sunday afternoon, July 13th 2025, we turned the studio into a small shop with soul for four hours. Three local brands joined us: Popiloni Ceramics with unique ceramics from Gdańsk, Maki Candles with candles and scents that set the mood, and Yoco Coffee running the bar. Music by alexnoisbit. Plenty of things to touch, smell and take home. The pop-up ran from 12:00 to 16:00 and continued the series we started with BVCLUB × KIOSK. The studio as a bar, a shop, a place where people from different worlds meet for one day.

frime-1-urodziny.title: FRIME 1ST BIRTHDAY
frime-1-urodziny.body: Studio's first birthday, July 18th 2026, right here at Wilcza 26. Hand-drawn poster, a bartender with two champagne glasses crossed into a letter V, drinks for everyone who shows up, music until late. We celebrate with clients, friends from the industry and the people who helped us launch. From three people and three stations a small local community grew, and we want to gather it under one roof. Detailed program and times go out on Instagram closer to the date.

next-event.title: Next event
next-event.body: We're working on the next pop-up. Details are being locked with the partner, the announcement lands on Instagram by the end of summer. To not miss it, follow @frime.studio.

common.not_found_desc: Maybe the address is wrong, maybe we lost something.

languages.title: We speak
languages.note: Book in any of these languages, we'll sort it out in the studio.

cosmetics.title: Cosmetics
cosmetics.lede: In the studio we work on two lines: Italian OWAY for washing, color and recovery, and British London Grooming for men's styling and beard care. We use both daily and only recommend them to clients for home.
cosmetics.buy_body: Some items are available in the studio after a visit. Ask the stylist who just washed your hair, or ask for a recommendation tailored to your hair type. We don't run a full shop yet, but we're preparing our own short FRIME line for 2027.
cosmetics.shop_soon_body: We're working on a short cosmetics line under the FRIME brand. Polish production, ingredient quality at OWAY level, a few items for washing and styling. Sign up via Instagram DM or leave your email during a visit and you'll be the first to know.

repigmentacja.title: Beard and head repigmentation
repigmentacja.lede: We fill in patchy beards and receding hairlines with a specialist pigment. It lasts a few weeks, fades naturally, leaves no hard edges. The treatment takes thirty minutes and costs 80 PLN.
repigmentacja.intro_body: Repigmentation is not a tattoo and not permanent makeup. We use a pigment that settles only in the upper skin layer, fades with normal skin shedding over a few weeks, and leaves no trace in deeper layers. It's a solution for anyone who wants to mask thinning or recession with no permanent commitment. After one visit you walk out with a visible result, after six to eight weeks the pigment fades and you choose whether to repeat.
repigmentacja.men_body: Our most frequent request. We fill in patches from scars, holes from old depilation or simply spots where the beard grows thinner. We match the pigment to the beard color drop by drop, so the final result is invisible from conversation distance. Up close it reads as a soft, natural beard trace.

men_variants.name: Spot fill
men_variants.desc: One or two areas, usually after a scar or removed mole. Takes 15-20 minutes, from 80 PLN.

men_variants.name: Full beard density
men_variants.desc: We work the whole beard to even out density. Thirty minutes, 80 PLN. The most picked option for medium-length beards.

men_variants.name: Post-surgery scar coverage
men_variants.desc: Pigment goes over the scar and around it, optically evening out the skin tone. Needs a gentler technique, we block 45 minutes.

repigmentacja.women_body: SMP, or scalp micropigmentation, is for men with a receding hairline and for people after a transplant who want to mask the surgery marks. The effect is an optical density boost on short hair or a hidden contrast between scalp and hair. It doesn't replace a transplant, it just makes short hair look denser.

women_variants.name: Hairline correction
women_variants.desc: We draw in a hairline that's naturally missing. Suits buzz cuts and very short hair where the scalp shows.

women_variants.name: Density boost
women_variants.desc: We cover areas with reduced hair density so the scalp doesn't reflect light. Immediate effect, works best on darker hair.

women_variants.name: FUE / FUT scar coverage
women_variants.desc: Linear scars from graft extraction get covered with pigment matched to surrounding skin. Requires an initial consultation, sometimes two sessions.

repigmentacja.visit_body: First a chat about how much you want to rebuild and how strong the look should be. We match the color on a small skin patch first so you see the shade before the full treatment. Work takes thirty minutes for a beard, up to an hour for a larger head area. Most people say the discomfort feels like tweezing. Before you leave you get a card with aftercare for the first seven days.
repigmentacja.care_body: First 48 hours no sauna, no pool, no strong sun. For a week avoid scrubbing the area, wash with a mild gel. Over the next days the pigment darkens slightly, then settles to its target shade. Full result shows after five to seven days. To keep the intensity, come back every six to eight weeks.
repigmentacja.price_value: from 80 PLN, larger areas up to 200 PLN

faq.q: Does it hurt?
faq.a: Most people say it's less intense than waxing and comparable to tweezing. The most sensitive spots are the nose and around the lips. We can apply a numbing cream before the treatment if you prefer.

faq.q: How long does it last?
faq.a: Four to eight weeks on a beard, six to ten weeks on the head. The fading speed depends on your skin type, sun exposure and how you wash the area.

faq.q: Does it leave any permanent mark?
faq.a: No. We use a pigment that sits only in the epidermis and fades with natural skin shedding. After some time you're back to the original state.

faq.q: Can I work out the same day?
faq.a: A calm walk yes, an intense session no. Sweat opens the fresh skin and washes out part of the pigment. Give yourself 24-48 hours of calm.

faq.q: Can I come in with a long beard and fill all of it?
faq.a: On a long beard the pigment makes little sense, you can't see it anyway. Repigmentation works best on beards up to 5 mm where the skin shows. If you plan to trim the beard short later, that's the perfect moment to start.

faq.q: How much for a full session?
faq.a: Standard visit is 80 PLN. Larger areas, scar coverage after a transplant or work in two sessions runs 150-200 PLN. We give a precise quote during the consultation, which is free.

repigmentacja.cta_text: The initial consultation is free. You can pair it with a wash and cut if you're already booking a visit.

krotkie-fryzury.title: Short women's cuts
krotkie-fryzury.lede: Pixie, bob, buzz cut, asymmetry, bowl. We do every short cut coming back in 2026, and the ones that never left. Visit takes 60 to 75 minutes, price from 150 to 250 PLN.
krotkie-fryzury.intro_body: A short women's cut isn't one style, it's a whole category with sharp differences between variants. Pixie and bob are the two pillars, but each holds dozens of sub-types: bowl, buzz, pixie with an undercut, choppy bob, bob with a fringe. At FRIME every visit starts with a quick chat about how a given style will sit on your hair texture and structure, because the difference between a beautiful and a disastrous result lives right there.
krotkie-fryzury.men_body: Pixie is a classic that never left, it just shifts proportions every few seasons. In 2026 we see more texture, more fringe, fewer slick versions. The women's buzz cut is making a comeback as a strong statement move, usually after long hair.

men_variants.name: Classic pixie
men_variants.desc: Short top, shorter sides, longer fringe. The most recognizable variant, sits well on finer hair because it adds optical volume.

men_variants.name: Pixie with undercut
men_variants.desc: Sides and back cut to skin, top stays longer, usually with a fringe. The edgy version, easy to push into a stronger look with the right product.

men_variants.name: Women's buzz cut
men_variants.desc: All one length, short, even, usually 3-6 mm. Least maintenance, most character. Needs a trim every three weeks.

men_variants.name: Modern bowl cut
men_variants.desc: Geometric fringe and rounded sides, in a more textural take than the classic bowl. A bit punk, a bit editorial.

krotkie-fryzury.women_body: The bob is the second pillar of short cuts and often the first step for someone coming off long hair. In 2026 the bob with a fringe and the asymmetric bob are leading. The classic blunt version holds its own.

women_variants.name: Classic bob
women_variants.desc: Length sits at the jawline, straight, no layers. A strong minimalist pick, great on thick straight hair.

women_variants.name: Bob with a fringe
women_variants.desc: Classic bob plus a full or lightly textural fringe. The most picked 2026 variant, the fringe frames the whole cut.

women_variants.name: Asymmetric bob
women_variants.desc: One side a few centimeters longer. Fits if you want a short cut but fear symmetry. More fashion forward than the classic.

women_variants.name: Choppy bob with layers
women_variants.desc: Torn ends, light layers, more movement. Works best on wavy hair, where layers reinforce the natural texture.

krotkie-fryzury.visit_body: First five minutes are about references and what will actually work. We show our portfolio, name the trade-offs. If you have specific Instagram or Pinterest pictures, bring them, it saves time. Wash, cut, optional product finish. A color consultation, if it comes into play, is always free.
krotkie-fryzury.care_body: Short cuts ask for regularity, not piles of product. Every three to four weeks come back for an outline trim, the length stays. Daily a light cream or wax does the job, depending on the style. Buzz cuts only need shampoo. Pixies and bobs with a fringe often need a separate fringe wash more often than the rest.

faq.q: Does a short cut age you?
faq.a: Not the cut itself, just wrong proportions. A pixie with a soft fringe can take off years, a pixie with a slick side parting can add some. That's why we consult first, cut after.

faq.q: Going from long, how long does it take?
faq.a: Usually an hour to an hour fifteen. If a lot of length is coming off, give yourself a beat between the decision and the visit. Many people prefer two stages two weeks apart to adjust.

faq.q: How long to grow back?
faq.a: Buzz to pixie 3-4 months, pixie to bob 6-8 months. Along the way it's worth coming in for trims to avoid awkward overgrown stages.

faq.q: Should I bring references?
faq.a: Yes, the more the better. The ideal set is 3-5 pictures showing what you like, plus 1-2 of what you don't want. Much easier to hit what you actually mean.

faq.q: Do you color in the same visit as a short cut?
faq.a: Color usually goes in a separate appointment, after the cut. Length and proportions affect how a shade reads. The color consultation is free and can be added to your visit at no extra cost.

faq.q: Will short hair really cut my daily styling time?
faq.a: Usually yes. A buzz cut is zero minutes, a pixie and most bobs run 2-5 minutes after the shower. Exception: heavily textural versions, which take 5-10 minutes with a bit of cream or wax.

krotkie-fryzury.cta_text: Fastest through Booksy. If you're planning a big switch from long to very short, book an hour fifteen and bring references.

mullet.title: Mullet at FRIME
mullet.lede: The mullet is back for real, and at our studio it sits in the top three weekly bookings. We do both the men's versions, the classic ones and the ones with a sharp fade, and the women's wolf cuts, which technically are mullets too, they just sound better in English.
mullet.intro_title: What the 2026 mullet is actually about
mullet.intro_body: A mullet means short on top and the sides, longer in the back. That rule has been around since the seventies, but the current wave looks nothing like the eighties versions or the football poster cuts. The contemporary mullet is more textural, less geometric, often curly, and much easier to maintain than it looks. In Warsaw it's worn across the wardrobe spectrum, so we keep it as one menu position without splitting it into men's and women's pricing.
mullet.men_body: Most people come in for the classic or the fade version. The whole visit takes an hour. We start with a chat about how much length you want to keep in the back and how sharp the side transition should be. On straight hair almost any variant works. On curly hair the softer variants without a hard fade tend to sit better.

men_variants.name: Classic mullet
men_variants.desc: A visible line between shorter top and longer back, no fade. The most recognizable version, works well on medium-density straight hair.

men_variants.name: Mullet with a fade
men_variants.desc: Sides cut to skin or very short, top stays textured, back stays long. The most picked variant in 2026, holds up well under a cap and makes a stronger first impression.

men_variants.name: Modern shag mullet
men_variants.desc: Looser, choppier, with a fringe falling onto the forehead. Almost indistinguishable from a nineties grunge musician's cut. Suits thick hair.

men_variants.name: Curly mullet
men_variants.desc: For naturally curly or wavy hair. Cut for actual curls, not a flat-ironed version. A bit of product at home and that's it.

mullet.women_body: On the women's side the same cut usually goes by wolf cut or shag. The rule stays, short on top and sides, longer in the back, but the proportions are different, fringes heavier, layers fuller. The visit runs sixty to seventy-five minutes depending on starting length.

women_variants.name: Wolf cut
women_variants.desc: Most picked women's variant. Strong layers around the face, longer ends in the back, fringe optional. Works on straight and lightly wavy hair.

women_variants.name: Soft mullet
women_variants.desc: A softer version without sharp transitions. A more feminine silhouette, a good move if it's the first time you're moving away from long hair.

women_variants.name: Curly mullet
women_variants.desc: Cut for actual curls, dry method. Needs someone who knows how to cut curly hair, which is us. No weird flat-iron tricks to fake volume.

women_variants.name: Mullet with a strong fringe
women_variants.desc: Full, wide fringe plus a mullet in the back. The editorial version, stands out hard. Needs upkeep, fringe trim every four weeks.

mullet.visit_body: Calm. First five minutes are a chat about what you want and what will actually work on your hair. We show references, name the trade-offs. Then wash, cut, optional product finish. For men's mullets we block an hour, for women's wolf cuts an hour to seventy-five minutes. Color or perm consultations, if they happen to come into play, are always free.
mullet.care_body: Despite the noise, a mullet doesn't need daily product. A stronger texture shampoo and a light paste or styling cream is enough. Every four to six weeks it's worth popping in for a side trim, the length in the back can grow longer. For curly hair, skip the brush, use fingers plus a curl cream.
mullet.price_value: from 150 PLN, men's 150-200 PLN, women's 150-250 PLN depending on length

faq.q: Will a mullet suit my face?
faq.a: Practically any face, if you pick the right side length and fringe. An oval face takes everything. A round face does better with a longer fringe and shorter sides. A longer face with a shorter top and a softer fade. That's why the consultation at the start of the visit matters.

faq.q: How long before a mullet grows out and stops looking right?
faq.a: Four to six weeks minimum, six to eight if your hair grows slower. After that it's usually worth popping in for a side trim and leaving the back to keep growing. Over time a mullet flows naturally into a shag or a longer cut without a sudden overgrown stage.

faq.q: Do you do wolf cuts on very curly hair?
faq.a: Yes, it's one of our favorite variants. We cut dry, meaning on dry hair after a wash, no flat-ironing. That way we see the real curl pattern and don't deliver a cut that looks different once it dries.

faq.q: Is the mullet actually unisex?
faq.a: The cutting rule yes. What differs is naming tradition. What men call a mullet, women often call a wolf cut, shag or modern mullet. Technically the same logic. In Booksy we pick the price line based on starting length, not gender.

faq.q: What does it cost?
faq.a: Men's version 150-200 PLN, women's 150-250 PLN depending on length. A mullet with a fade fits inside the same range with no extra charge for the fade. A consultation, if you happen to be considering color, is free.

faq.q: Can I come in for a quick fix if someone else did my mullet badly?
faq.a: Yes, we regularly rescue what went wrong elsewhere. Sometimes it can be fixed on the spot, sometimes you have to wait for it to grow and rebuild from there. After a short consultation we tell you honestly what can be done now.

mullet.cta_text: Fastest through Booksy. During the week we often fill two to three days ahead, weekends a week.

srodmiescie_poludniowe.title: FRIME in Śródmieście Południowe
srodmiescie_poludniowe.lede: We sit in the middle of the busiest stretch of Warsaw. One block off Marszałkowska, a short walk from Plac Konstytucji, Hala Koszyki and Łazienki Park. Wilcza 26 is our address from day one and we're not planning to move.

sections.title: Where to find us exactly
sections.body: Wilcza is a short but dense street that runs from Marszałkowska to Mokotowska. We're at number 26, ground floor, in one of those pre-war townhouses that survived the war and still carry the whole block. Shopfront facing the street, blue logo on the door, three stations inside. If you're in doubt, look for the big navy FRIME sign.

sections.title: How to get here
sections.body: By metro, the fastest is line M1 from Centrum, exit toward Plac Defilad, then walk south down Marszałkowska. Fifteen minutes. Politechnika (also M1) is even closer, twelve minutes through Plac Konstytucji. Trams 4, 18 and 35 stop at Krucza, one turn into Wilcza. Bus 107 runs along Hoża parallel to us, get off at Krucza. For drivers, the best bet is to park on Mokotowska or in the paid garage under Hala Koszyki. The whole area is paid-parking zone and spots free up slowly, count on fifteen extra minutes for hunting.

sections.title: What's around
sections.body: Five minutes on foot you have Plac Zbawiciela with its cafes and the Charlotte bakery, then Mokotowska with concept stores and third-wave coffee. Plac Trzech Krzyży with its boutiques, restaurants and the flagship Wedel chocolate shop is seven minutes away. Hala Koszyki, the restored 1908 market hall with a food court and the Mąka i Woda pizzeria, is ten minutes east. Plac Konstytucji with its socialist-realist MDM architecture is our nearest large landmark. Łazienki Park, with the pond, peacocks and the Palace on the Water, is twenty minutes on foot or seven by bike. On Wilcza and the neighboring Hoża, Wspólna and Piękna you'll find pizzerias, small bars, two bookshops, a few galleries and the kind of quiet cafes where you can wait for your visit or stay for lunch after.

sections.title: Who our neighbors and clients are
sections.body: Śródmieście Południowe is a mix you can't reduce to a single type. Students from the Politechnika, office people from Rondo Daszyńskiego and Mokotów, artists who didn't move out to Praga, and residents of the renovated apartments in old townhouses. All of them drop in. Someone before a meeting, someone after work, someone before an evening on Mokotowska. Sometimes tourists from the nearby hotels or people in town for a conference at the Palace of Culture. That's why we run a unisex model, why consultations are free, and why Booksy regularly fills up a week ahead.

sections.title: First time in the area
sections.body: If you've never been to Śródmieście Południowe, it's easy to pair the visit with something else. Breakfast on Mokotowska, we cut your hair, then a walk to Łazienki or a chocolate at Wedel on Plac Trzech Krzyży. Evenings the same route works backwards: drink at Hala Koszyki, then haircut, then dinner on Hoża. Three hours and you have a new look plus a good day in the city.

srodmiescie_poludniowe.cta_title: Booksy is the fastest way in
srodmiescie_poludniowe.cta_text: Address: Wilcza 26, 00-544 Warsaw, Śródmieście Południowe. In peak season we fill up a few days ahead, so booking in advance saves time.

cookies_banner.title: Cookies
cookies_banner.body: This site uses only essential cookies needed for basic functionality (such as remembering your language choice). No tracking analytics, no advertising cookies.
